assertions.expectation: Explains that Extended channel provides up to 24 months of support vs 14 months for standard channels
assertions.reason: Response clearly states '24-month support lifecycle (vs 14 months for Regular/Rapid)'

assertions.expectation: Mentions that Extended channel has additional cost (it's not free)
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions 'Higher cost - Extended channel has additional pricing' and provides specific cost estimate of ~$0.10/hour per cluster

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended channel gets the same versions as Regular but keeps them available longer
assertions.reason: Response doesn't explicitly explain that Extended gets the same versions as Regular but maintains them longer - it only mentions quarterly releases and delayed features

assertions.expectation: Warns that staying on older versions longer means missing new features and accumulating technical debt
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Delayed feature access - New Kubernetes features arrive later' as a con, addressing the technical debt concern

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel to Extended
assertions.reason: Response provides the specific command: 'gcloud container clusters update CLUSTER_NAME --release-channel extended --region YOUR_REGION'

assertions.expectation: Advises checking that the current version (1.29) is available on Extended before switching
assertions.reason: Response includes checking version compatibility with 'gcloud container get-server-config --region=YOUR_REGION' and discusses checking Extended channel availability

assertions.expectation: Recommends Extended for environments with compliance requirements or slow internal change processes
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Compliance friendly - Easier to maintain certified environments' and emphasizes production stability benefits

assertions.expectation: Mentions that Extended doesn't mean you can skip upgrades forever — you still must upgrade within 24 months
assertions.reason: While the response implies ongoing maintenance through various upgrade commands and maintenance windows, it doesn't explicitly warn that upgrades are still required within the 24-month window
